import cv2
from PIL import Image, ImageFilter

def preprocess_image(image_path, resize_size=256, crop_size=224, grayscale=False, blur_radius=2, contrast_limit=0.8):
    """Preprocess an image for a computer vision task using OpenCV and Pillow."""
    # Load image
    image = cv2.imread(image_path)
    if image is None:
        raise ValueError(f"Image at {image_path} could not be loaded.")

    # Convert to RGB for consistency
    image = cv2.cvtColor(image, cv2.COLOR_BGR2RGB)

    # Resize image
    image = cv2.resize(image, (resize_size, resize_size))

    # Grayscale if requested
    if grayscale:
        image = cv2.cvtColor(image, cv2.COLOR_RGB2GRAY)

    # Apply blur if requested
    if blur_radius > 0:
        image = cv2.GaussianBlur(image, (blur_radius, blur_radius), 0)

    # Contrast adjustment
    if contrast_limit > 0:
        image = cv2.convertScaleAbs(image, alpha=contrast_limit, beta=0)

    # Crop to specified size
    image = cv2.resize(image, (crop_size, crop_size))

    # Save processed image (optional)
    processed_path = f"processed_{image_path}"
    cv2.imwrite(processed_path, image)

    return image

# Example usage
if __name__ == "__main__":
    image_path = "input_image.jpg"
    preprocessed_image = preprocess_image(image_path)
    print("Preprocessing complete.")
